_comment: Video pool for the daily social autoposter. pick_daily_video.py rotates through `pool`; video_publisher.py posts and then RETIRES each posted clip (removes its entry here and deletes the MP4). Refresh the pool from the factory vault (~/videos/_vault).

pool.caption: Junk drawer audit: old earbuds $40, smartwatch $90, forgotten tablet $150, last phone $250, retired console $430. That drawer is a paycheck. purchasingcorp.com
PC gamers: your old GPU basically buys the new one. Used cards bring up to $680, paid same day. Sell the old card, cover the upgrade — that's the loop. purchasingcorp.com
That old iPhone in your drawer is worth real cash. Any model, any condition — we paid $635 the same day. Quote in 60 seconds, cash before bedtime. purchasingcorp.com

That golden goal has everyone hosting watch parties — and suddenly shopping. Before you spend, sell: the average home has $500 of unused tech sitting in drawers. Cash before kickoff at purchasingcorp.com
Physical vs digital, round one: digital resale value is zero, forever. A disc console holds up to $380. Physical wins — cash the win at purchasingcorp.com
Cracked screen, dead battery, three years old — guess what we paid for it. $50? Higher. $100? Higher. $190, for a phone with a shattered screen. Guess yours, then check for real: purchasingcorp.com
Your old MacBook loses ~$25 in value every month it sits. Sell in July: $480. Wait till October: $400. Same laptop — only the date changed. Lock today's price at purchasingcorp.com
Gratitude list, tech drawer edition: the old phone $250, the forgotten tablet $150, the watch + earbuds $130. That's $530 of blessings in one drawer. Count yours at purchasingcorp.com
You give this phone 3 hours a day. The last one? Face-down in a drawer earning $0, 24 hours a day. Average payout for a retired phone: $340. Give us 60 seconds of your scroll. purchasingcorp.com
Spain: 6 World Cup games, zero goals allowed. Your tech drawer: 6 years, zero dollars produced. One streak is legendary — the other has $500 waiting to get on the board. purchasingcorp.com
